panels.description: Medium shot: The Duchess, with an oversized head and a severe, impatient expression, looks down at Alice. Alice is gesturing earnestly with her hands, trying to explain the Earth's rotation. The background is a smoky Victorian kitchen with copper pots.

bubbles.text: Which would _not_ be an advantage, just think of what work it would make with the day and night! You see the earth takes twenty-four hours to turn round on its axis—

panels.description: Close-up: The Duchess's face is contorted in a sudden, manic outburst. Her eyes are wide and bulging, teeth bared. She points a sharp finger directly at Alice. Low angle to emphasize her threatening presence, heavy shadows under her brow.

bubbles.text: Talking of axes, chop off her head!

panels.description: Medium close shot: Alice looks flustered and confused, hand to her mouth as she re-calculates her figures. Beside her, the Duchess is turning away dismissively, a look of pure annoyance on her face as she waves her hand at Alice.

bubbles.text: Twenty-four hours, I _think_; or is it twelve? I—
Oh, don't bother _me_, I never could abide figures!

panels.description: Wide shot: The Duchess is seated, nursing a bundle that is clearly a baby, but she is shaking it violently at the end of every line she sings. Smoke swirls thick in the air. The Duchess has a wide, frightening grin.

bubbles.text: Speak roughly to your little boy, And beat him when he sneezes: He only does it to annoy, Because he knows it teases. Wow! wow! wow!

panels.description: Action wide shot: The Duchess tosses the baby through the air toward Alice. In the background, the Cook is seen in mid-swing, throwing a large frying pan toward the Duchess as she exits through a door. Dynamic action lines show the trajectories.

bubbles.text: Here! you may nurse it a bit, if you like! I must go and get ready to play croquet with the Queen,

panels.description: Medium shot: Alice is struggling to hold the 'baby', which is twisting into a knot and kicking its legs. Alice is outside now, the forest in the background. She looks down at the creature with a mixture of concern and anxiety.

bubbles.text: If I don't take this child away with me, they're sure to kill it in a day or two: wouldn't it be murder to leave it behind?

panels.description: Close-up: Alice peers directly into the baby's face. The baby's nose is clearly turning into a flat pig snout, and its eyes are small and beady. It grunts audibly. Alice's face shows dawning horror and disgust.
